The Amazon rainforest, often referred to as the lungs of the Earth, spans over 5.5 million square kilometers across nine countries in South America. It is home to approximately 10% of all species on Earth, including over 40,000 plant species, 1,300 bird species, and more than 3,000 types of fish. Deforestation remains a critical threat, with an estimated 17% of the forest lost in the last 50 years.

Quantum computing leverages quantum mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computations that would be infeasible for classical computers.
